posts.author: Sanu_George
posts.text: Successful market penetration into product and service segments dominated by established European and American companies is a well‑proven strategy in microcap investing. This company aligns strongly with that theme and shows clear potential to benefit from it.

posts.author: Apurva_Dubey
posts.text: @thakurvi I and bought in May-June 2022 when the mcap was in the 250-300 Cr range. Below was my thesis (I had shared it with a friend in June 2023 – the stock didn’t make any move in my 1st year of holding it). I still believe in my thesis except for the triggers (there are new ones now). I’ve only added more in the last 4 years on major dips. My core belief is 21st century will be the century of biology/gene (despite AI dominating the narrative right now). After many false starts over the last 40 years the whip is in the hands of biology. I had read this book by Jamie Metzl where he had this…

posts.author: thakurvi
posts.text: Hi…Apurva , I am studying 3B blackbio. Business of 3B blackbio is very difficult to understand for me. Molecular diagnostics is very difficult to understand. Moreover US market is still atleast faraway 2 years if everything goes right due to usfda regulation. What attracted you in this company as a retail investor? Will love to hear you views.

posts.author: Apurva_Dubey
posts.text: Another datapoint: i follow Ian Cassel (he is a legendary microcap investor). Of late, he has been liking posts of a company called PRECIPO Dx – it’s a listed US company with a mcap of $40M+ They have some overlap with 3B’s portfolio – for example in this ad below you can see they are promoting BCR-ABL1 mutation test (which is also a flagship offering from 3B). The intresting part is: PRECIPO has been EBIDTA negative in 10 of 13 past quarters. Screenshot_20260715-142117 2400×1080 190 KB Screenshot_20260715-073829~2 1005×1646 154 KB

posts.author: Apurva_Dubey
posts.text: One thing that was bothering me was why did they buy an EBIDTA negative company (Coris). So I went looking for sub $20M mcap listed Dx companies in the US. It turns out that all of them are either PAT negative or pre-revenue (at least the ones Gemini Pro found for me). This looks like a tough business to operate - where folks at the top (Thermofisher, Qiagen capture majority of the profit pool). It looks like Tech, where the winner (or the duopoly) takes all! image 1162×279 20.9 KB

posts.author: Apurva_Dubey
posts.text: @enelay Agappe and 3B is not a fair comparison. If anything, 3B’s products/offerings are more sophisticated, commanding a higher margin…and IMHO 3B should command a better valuation. Disc.: invested in 3B, and biased. posts.author: enelay
posts.text: 1. Potential PE deal happening in unlisted IVD company Agappe Diagnostics. 650 cr revenue, 100-120 cr EBITDA, 50-60 cr PAT for FY27e - valuation may be 3,000 cr so 60x forward multiple. image 1290×920 414 KB 2. June SHP shows some incremental shopping by promoter, and longtime HNI holders Varun Daga (5.53% to 5.62%) and Maj Navraj Singh Grewal (2.6% to 2.65%) - both of these investors bought in below 100 levels pre Covid. More recent investor Prajesh Maroo has trimmed/exited his 2.22% stake to below 1%.

posts.author: deepakreads
posts.text: Going by the past promoters actions and talks looks like genuine buy and not just to prep the stock price in bad times. Also I was comparing to molbio diagnostics. Molbio has impressive portfolio of innovative products. Some by acquisition like portable xrays machine. Not sure of the price they might have paid. I remember the talk of 3bblackio promoters where they emphasize on the ROI and correct buying price. So one company might look attractive and impressive on the list of products but it is also important to have positive financial return.

posts.author: enelay
posts.text: Based on the concall and past performance, the way I am thinking about FY27 EBITDA margin is - 3B BlackBio (ex-Coris) - 50% margin (similar to FY25). This business will be 70% of consol FY27 revenue (~120 cr) Coris - 0% margin (guided). This business will be 30% of consol FY27 revenue (~55 cr) This would imply a blended EBITDA margin expectation of 35% for full year FY27. Margins will be lower in H1 (even lower than Q4) and H2 will be better. Absolute cash profits should be higher year on year despite lower margin.

posts.author: apexcapital
posts.text: There was no such bullish guidance regarding growth. It was just one person on the call trying to put words into management’s mouth while management made it clear multiple times that on a consolidated basis, they are looking at 15-20% topline growth and overall EBITDA margins would come down due to Coris being a negative EBITDA margin company still. It wasn’t clear whether on consolidated basis, 3B margins would be 25% (same as Q4) or 40% (same as FY26) going ahead into FY27. So more realistic expectation would be FY27 rev: 165 Cr @17 % growth EBITDA: 65 Cr @40 % margin PAT: ~60 Cr (including…
